title: Chromolaena minasgeraesensis
taxonomy: Chromolaena minasgeraesensis is a species within the flowering plant family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a member of the genus Chromolaena, which is a group of flowering plants native to the Americas. The genus Chromolaena is further classified under the subfamily Asteroideae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Within the subfamily Asteroideae, Chromolaena minasgeraesensis is a part of the tribe Eupatorieae, which includes various species of plants native to the Americas. Further classification places Chromolaena minasgeraesensis within the order Asterales.
uses: Chromolaena minasgeraesensis, a plant native to South America, has been studied for its potential medicinal uses. Research has shown that extracts from this plant may possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Traditional medicine practices in some regions have utilized Chromolaena minasgeraesensis to treat various health conditions, including fever and skin infections. However, more in-depth studies are necessary to fully understand the plant's therapeutic applications. Further investigation into the bioactive compounds present in Chromolaena minasgeraesensis may lead to the discovery of new medicinal uses.

The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage.
